name: Digital's VAX: Alive and Kicking With TPC Benchmarks
description: This Viewpoint section analyzes Digital Equipment Corporation's March 1992 VAX product-line price-performance realignment, using TPC-A benchmark data to show that Digital simultaneously rationalized pricing across its entire VAX range—from MicroVAX 3100 to VAX 6000—while achieving the industry's best price-performance at the entry level. Aberdeen concludes that Digital has deliberately repositioned the VAX as a commercial price-performance leader, tripling cost-efficiency since 1990 and becoming competitive not only with HP and IBM but also with emerging RISC vendors such as Sequent.
